item.title: Ex-prosecutor allegedly stole secret report on Trump, named file "Bundt Cake Recipe"
item.description: A former federal prosecutor was charged this week with emailing herself a report on the Justice Department's investigation into President Trump that a judge had kept under lock and key, under the file name "Bundt_Cake_Recipe.pdf."

item.title: High gas prices drive Georgia man to create a "mini car," costing about $3 to fill up
item.description: In response to high gas prices, 30-year-old Mali Hightower, a handyman near Atlanta, said he created a "mini car" that costs about $3 to fill up. Tony Dokoupil has the story.

item.title: Vanessa Trump announces breast cancer diagnosis
item.description: Vanessa Trump, 48, was married to Donald Trump Jr. for 12 years. They share five children together.

item.title: U.S. intel community analyzing how Cuba might respond to military action
item.description: These kinds of intelligence forecasts attempt not only to show the immediate consequences of an American action, but the chain of reactions that may follow.

item.title: Vanessa Trump announces breast cancer diagnosis
item.description: Vanessa Trump announced on Instagram that she has been diagnosed with breast cancer and is working with her doctors on a treatment plan.

item.title: TikTok star accused of plotting to murder former boy band singer amid custody battle
item.description: A social media influencer and her father are accused of plotting to kill her famous singer ex, amid a bitter years-long custody dispute, court documents show. Jonathan Vigliotti is following the case.

item.title: Hybrid car explodes, catches fire in New York City
item.description: A car near Wall Street in New York City burst into flames on Tuesday night. Tom Hanson has an update from lower Manhattan.

item.title: Trump flexes political muscle in Republican primary races
item.description: President Trump's favored candidates won their primaries on Tuesday night and his Republican critics did not. But the president's approval ratings continue to slip, now just six months out from the midterm elections. Weijia Jiang reports.

item.title: Justice Department indicts Raúl Castro as Trump threatens a "friendly takeover" of Cuba
item.description: President Trump said that the U.S. is "freeing up Cuba" after the Justice Department indicted its former leader, Raúl Castro, on charges including murder. Margaret Brennan has the details.

item.title: Sinkhole opens near LaGuardia runway ahead of Memorial Day weekend
item.description: A record 45 million Americans are preparing to travel over Memorial Day weekend. Kris Van Cleave reports on dramatic incidents creating headaches for people at airports and on the rails ahead of the travel weekend.

item.title: What Nvidia's Q1 earnings report says about state of AI race
item.description: Cementing its status as the world's most valuable company, Nvidia reported record revenue in its latest earnings report. Britney Nguyen, Nvidia and chips reporter at MarketWatch, joins with analysis.

item.title: Ally of DOJ pardon attorney seeks to join board of Trump's $1.7+ billion fund
item.description: In his application letter, attorney Mike Howell proposed organizing a national gathering of "thousands of victims of weaponization," including those involved in the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riot.

item.title: Trump backs Ken Paxton against incumbent Sen. John Cornyn in Texas
item.description: President Trump has announced his support for Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton, who is challenging incumbent Sen. John Cornyn, in the state's Republican primary. Trump-backed challengers have already successfully defeated at least two incumbent candidates targeted by the president. CBS News' Fin Gómez reports.

item.title: NAACP calls on athletes, fans to boycott NCAA sports programs in 8 states over voting rights
item.description: The NAACP is calling on Black student athletes and fans to boycott public universities in several Southern states over voting rights. LZ Granderson, opinion columnist at the Los Angeles Times, joins to discuss.

item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: Reagan's 1984 landslide reelection victory
item.description: President Ronald Reagan's 1984 victory over Democratic nominee Walter Mondale is the largest Electoral College victory in modern politics. Major Garrett introduces CBS News Radio's coverage of the historic results.

item.title: U.S. announces Ebola response assistance
item.description: The World Health Organization said the Ebola outbreak in the Congo has been circulating for at least two months and will likely continue to grow. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has more information.

item.title: GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy takes digs at Trump, urging renewed unity in politics
item.description: Cassidy, who lost his reelection bid last week, called for leaders who are "steady, not erratic" and "thoughtful, not impulsive."

item.title: Harvard faculty votes to limit number of A's awarded
item.description: Harvard University faculty members voted to cap the number of A's awarded to students in an effort to make the grades more meaningful.

item.title: SpaceX files for IPO as rocket company preps for market debut
item.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX is moving ahead with plans to go public in what some expect will be the biggest IPO ever.

item.title: How the Trump administration is putting pressure on Cuba
item.description: The Justice Department has charged former Cuban President Raúl Castro in the latest point of pressure on the island nation. CBS News intelligence and national security reporter Olivia Gazis has more.

item.title: Trump says Netanyahu will do "whatever I want him to do" on Iran
item.description: Asked what he said to Israel's leader about a decision to hold off on new Iran strikes, Trump said Netanyahu will "do whatever I want him to do."

item.title: Lawmakers react to Trump's IRS settlement
item.description: President Trump's settlement with the IRS is getting mixed reactions from some Republicans and fierce pushback from Democrats. Former Republican Rep. Peter Meijer of Michigan and Democratic strategist Cameron French join with analysis.

item.title: Sen. Jacky Rosen reacts to comments from Massie and Galindo, unpacks her bill to fight antisemitism
item.description: Democratic Sen. Jacky Rosen of Nevada and Republican Sen. James Lankford of Oklahoma have introduced a bill to strengthen federal efforts to fight antisemitism. Rosen joins "The Takeout" to discuss the legislation.

item.title: Keisha Lance Bottoms says her margin of victory in Georgia shows "we have a very powerful campaign"
item.description: Former Atlanta Mayor Keisha Lance Bottoms won the Democratic gubernatorial primary in Georgia on Tuesday night. Lance Bottoms joins "The Takeout" to unpack her victory and look forward to the general election.

item.title: Byron Allen on "Comics Unleashed" as it moves to new time slot on CBS
item.description: Comedian and media mogul Byron Allen brings "Comics Unleashed" to CBS's late night slot, replacing "The Late Show With Stephen Colbert."

item.title: Highest gas prices in years expected to continue rising this summer
item.description: U.S. motorists are likely to face even hotter gas prices as the summer driving season kicks off, according to a new analysis.

item.title: U.S. indicts Cuba's Raúl Castro for murder over downing of planes in 1996
item.description: Former Cuban leader Raúl Castro was indicted by a U.S. grand jury in connection with the Cuban military's fatal downing of two planes in 1996 — an escalation in the U.S. pressure campaign against the Cuban government.

item.title: 2 officers in Jan. 6 riot sue to block DOJ "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: The $1.776 billion fund, which is part of the agreement to settle Trump's lawsuit against the IRS and Treasury Dept., is to be used to compensate those who claim that the government weaponized the legal system against them.

item.title: The major cases the Supreme Court will decide in the coming weeks
item.description: The Supreme Court's term is set to end around the end of June, with decisions on birthright citizenship, transgender athlete bans and gun rights still to come.

item.title: Trump says he was "an hour away" from striking Iran
item.description: President Trump says he scrapped a planned attack on Iran at the request of Gulf allies as "serious negotiations" on a peace deal are underway.
